# MOTION.md
# Preset: Default — balanced
# Generated by: npx motion-md init
# Drop this file in your project root.
# AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) read this before generating animated components.
# Edit values directly or regenerate at: https://craftedby.ai

---
motion:
  enter:
    duration: 300ms # medium2
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    direction: rise
    distance: 16px
---

## Enter

Elements enter with presence and intent — the easing determines whether they arrive softly or spring into place.

---
motion:
  modal:
    duration: 350ms # medium3
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    direction: scale
    distance: 12px
---

## Modal

Modals arrive deliberately and dismiss quickly — the exit is always faster than the enter.

---
motion:
  toast:
    duration: 300ms # medium2
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    slide-from: top
    distance: 32px
---

## Toast

Toasts interrupt briefly — fast enough to feel responsive, slow enough to be readable.

---
motion:
  notification:
    duration: 500ms # long2
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)
    slide-from: top
    distance: 48px
    exit-duration: 280ms
---

## Notification

Notifications should arrive with energy — they're interrupting the user for a reason.
The spring overshoot makes the card feel alive, like it demands to be noticed.
It springs up from below and lands with a satisfying bounce that says "hey, look at me."

---
motion:
  drawer:
    duration: 400ms # medium4
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    slide-from: right
---

## Drawer

Panels slide deliberately — opening a new context requires spatial commitment.

---
motion:
  press:
    duration: 120ms # short2
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)
    scale-down: 0.97
---

## Press

Press feedback is instant — the button confirms it heard you before any action fires.

---
motion:
  hover:
    duration: 150ms # short3
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1)
    scale: 1.03
---

## Hover

Hover is a whisper — just enough to confirm interactivity without demanding attention.

---
motion:
  stagger:
    item-duration: 250ms
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    direction: even
    stagger-delay: 40ms
    item-distance: 12px
---

## Stagger

Items cascade in — each one slightly behind the last, creating a sense of flow.

---
motion:
  error:
    duration: 150ms # short3
    easing: linear
    distance: 6px
    repeat: 2
---

## Error

Shake is unambiguous — linear motion reads as mechanical, which is exactly right for an error.

---
motion:
  skeleton:
    duration: 1200ms # long4
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1)
    pulse-range: 35% to 85%
---

## Skeleton

Slow, calm pulse — signals loading without creating urgency.

---
component-overrides:
  # Framer Motion — paste these transition objects directly into your components
  # Never hardcode duration or cubic-bezier values — always import from motion-tokens
  framer.drawer:
    initial: '{ x: "100%" }'
    animate: '{ x: 0, transition: { duration: 0.4, ease: [0.05,0.7,0.1,1] } }'
    exit:    '{ x: "100%", transition: { duration: 0.24 } }'
  framer.modal:
    initial: '{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.96 }'
    animate: '{ opacity: 1, scale: 1, transition: { duration: 0.35, ease: [0.05,0.7,0.1,1] } }'
    exit:    '{ opacity: 0, transition: { duration: 0.17 } }'
  framer.listItem:
    initial: '{ opacity: 0, x: -12 }'
    animate: '{ opacity: 1, x: 0, transition: { duration: 0.25, ease: [0.05,0.7,0.1,1] } }'
  framer.press:
    whileTap: '{ scale: 0.97, transition: { duration: 0.12, ease: [0.34,1.56,0.64,1] } }'
  framer.notification:
    initial: '{ opacity: 0, y: 48 }'
    animate: '{ opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.5, ease: [0.34,1.56,0.64,1] } }'
    exit:    '{ opacity: 0, y: 24, transition: { duration: 0.28 } }'
---
